title: The water tank on the roof
summary: Describe a rooftop water tank that collects rain and explain how a simple idea saves water every day.
topic: A rooftop water tank that collects and stores rainwater
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a water tank on a roof (one that collects rain, or one that stores water for the house). Say where it is, how it works in simple words, and how it helps your family save water. Use present simple, past simple, and 'and', 'but', 'because'.
hintStarters: On the roof of our house there is a…
We use this water for… because…
sampleText: On the roof of our house there is a big blue water tank. My father put it there five years ago. When it rains, the water runs down the roof into a pipe. The pipe carries the water into the tank. The tank stores the rain for many days. We do not use this water for drinking, but we use it for many other things. We water the garden with it and we wash the floor with it. In the dry season the tap water is sometimes weak. So the tank water helps us a lot. Last summer there was no rain for three weeks. But our tank was still half full. My mother was very happy because we did not waste any clean tap water on the plants. The tank is simple and it has no machine. It only needs the rain and a pipe. I like this idea because it saves water and money. I think every house with a roof can collect rain like this.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một bồn nước to màu xanh dương
Bố tôi đặt nó ở đó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the water runs down the roof
carries the water into the tank
stores the rain for many days
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trữ nước mưa trong nhiều ngày

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the tap water is sometimes weak
there was no rain for three weeks

nó tiết kiệm nước và tiền

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for how it works, past simple for last summer
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn để giải thích cách bồn nước hoạt động ('the water runs down the roof', 'the tank stores the rain') và quá khứ đơn để kể chuyện đã xảy ra ('My father put it there', 'there was no rain for three weeks'). Đừng trộn lẫn hai thì.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple to explain how the tank works ('the water runs down the roof', 'the tank stores the rain') and the past simple for past events ('My father put it there', 'there was no rain for three weeks'). Keep the tenses separate.

sampleTranslationVi: Trên mái nhà chúng tôi có một bồn nước to màu xanh dương. Bố tôi đặt nó ở đó năm năm trước. Khi trời mưa, nước chảy xuống mái nhà vào một ống nước. Ống nước dẫn nước vào bồn. Bồn trữ nước mưa trong nhiều ngày. Chúng tôi không dùng nước này để uống, nhưng dùng nó cho nhiều việc khác. Chúng tôi tưới vườn bằng nó và lau sàn bằng nó. Vào mùa khô nước máy đôi khi yếu. Nên nước trong bồn giúp chúng tôi rất nhiều. Mùa hè năm ngoái không có mưa trong ba tuần. Nhưng bồn của chúng tôi vẫn còn đầy một nửa. Mẹ tôi rất vui vì chúng tôi không phí nước máy sạch cho cây cối. Bồn nước đơn giản và nó không có máy móc. Nó chỉ cần mưa và một ống nước. Tôi thích ý tưởng này vì nó tiết kiệm nước và tiền. Tôi nghĩ mọi ngôi nhà có mái đều có thể hứng nước mưa như thế này.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'When it rains, the water ___ down the roof.'
options: run
running
ran
runs
mcQuestions.explanation: 'the water' is third-person singular, so the verb takes -s: 'runs'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for five years ago?
options: My father put it there five years ago.
My father putted it there five years ago.
My father put it there five years ago yesterday.
My father is put it there five years ago.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'put' has the same form in the past simple, and there is no time conflict.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Put' does not change in the past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' correctly to show a contrast?
options: We do not use it for drinking, but the garden we use it for it.
We do not use it for drinking, but we use it for the garden.
We not use it drinking, but for the garden we it use.
But we do not use it for drinking we use it the garden.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two full clauses, each with subject + verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Both clauses after 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'The tank ___ the rain for many days.'
options: store
storing
stores
stored
mcQuestions.explanation: 'the tank' is third-person singular, so the verb takes -s: 'stores'.
mcQuestions.hint: The subject is 'the tank' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple about last summer?
options: Last summer there were no rain for three weeks.
Last summer there is no rain for three weeks.
Last summer there was no rain for three week.
Last summer there was no rain for three weeks.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'there was' (singular, uncountable 'rain') and plural 'weeks' are correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'rain' countable here, and is it 'weeks' or 'week'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why your mother was happy?
options: My mother was happy because we did not waste any tap water.
My mother was happy because no tap water waste we.
My mother was happy so we tap water did not waste.
Because no waste tap water my mother happy was.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by a full past-simple clause: 'we did not waste any tap water'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because' you need a full clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds the most natural describing the tank?
options: The tank is simple and it have no machine.
The tank is simple and it has no machine.
The tank are simple and it has no machine.
The tank is simply and it has no machine.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'is simple' (adjective) and 'it has' (third-person 'has') are both correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Check 'is' + adjective and the 'it' form of 'have'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural and on-topic?
options: We waters the garden with it.
We water the gardens with its.
We water the garden with it.
We water the garden with it download.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'we water' (plural, no -s) and 'with it' are correct and natural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why you like the idea?
options: I like this idea because save water money it.
I like this idea so water and money saves.
Because save water money I like this idea it.
I like this idea because it saves water and money.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by a full clause: 'it saves water and money'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural opinion at the end?
options: Every house with a roof can collect rain like this.
Every house with a roof can collects rain like this.
Every houses with a roof can collect rain like this.
Every house with a roof can to collect rain like this.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'can collect' (modal + base verb) with singular 'every house' is correct.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'can', use the base verb, and 'every house' is singular.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Many simple ideas, like collecting rain on a roof, need no electricity at all. Why do you think people often forget these simple solutions and look for complicated ones?
